chat.content: Under $100, the Santal & Fig Candle ($92) is the cleanest gift: substantial, beautifully wrapped, and still comfortably inside the ceiling. If you want something smaller, the Ceramic Ring Dish ($35) has the same considered feel without pretending to be the main event.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → Curator + the-gift-table → find_pieces_hybrid (price filter + rerank)

toolCalls.description: Hybrid search for giftable pieces under $100
toolCalls.sql:
WITH candidates AS (
  SELECT product_id, name, price
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE price < 100 AND quantity > 0
),
vector_results AS (
  SELECT product_id, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector) AS vector_rank
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM candidates)
  LIMIT 20
),
fts_results AS (
  SELECT product_id, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY ts_rank_cd(description_tsv, $2::tsquery) DESC) AS text_rank
  FROM product_catalog
  WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM candidates)
    AND description_tsv @@ $2::tsquery
  LIMIT 20
)
SELECT COALESCE(v.product_id, f.product_id) AS product_id
FROM vector_results v
FULL OUTER JOIN fts_results f USING (product_id)
LIMIT 30;
toolCalls.resultSummary: Under-$100 gift pool reranked to Santal & Fig Candle, then Ceramic Ring Dish

memoryPills.content: Buys for others; explicit budgets; gift-ready, artisanal palette

telemetry.title: Memory Recall – Semantic
telemetry.description: Loaded Anna's gift-buyer profile from AgentCore Memory: explicit budgets, gift-ready artisanal palette. Used to weight under-$100 candidates.

telemetry.title: vector branch
telemetry.description: pgvector cosine over price-filtered gift candidates
telemetry.sql:
SELECT product_id, name, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog
WHERE price < 100 AND quantity > 0
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 20;

telemetry.title: Postgres FTS branch
telemetry.description: literal price and gift language over description_tsv
telemetry.sql:
SELECT product_id, name, price, ts_rank_cd(description_tsv, q) AS text_rank
FROM product_catalog, to_tsquery('english', 'beautiful:* | gift:*') q
WHERE price < 100 AND quantity > 0 AND description_tsv @@ q
ORDER BY text_rank DESC
LIMIT 20;

telemetry.title: RRF merge
telemetry.description: Fused vector and FTS branches under the $100 ceiling
telemetry.sql:
SELECT product_id, rrf_score
FROM rrf
ORDER BY rrf_score DESC
LIMIT 30;

telemetry.title: cohere.rerank-v3-5:0
telemetry.description: Reranked by gift suitability and price fit

telemetry.title: Memory Write – Working
telemetry.description: Wrote turn-scoped context to AgentCore Memory: looking for something beautiful under $100.

sections.title: The constraint
paragraphs: The price ceiling is not a final formatting step. It shapes retrieval first, then rerank decides which affordable items still feel like a gift.
